Features

01

### Short-term trend

Fit a rolling regression over a configurable window of recent weeks to
classify each period as increasing, not increasing, or decreasing,
following the Benedetti (2019) analytics strategy.

02

### Baseline detection

Compare current counts against a historical baseline using a
hierarchical log-linear model, with forecast values and prediction
intervals returned as new columns alongside the original data.

03

### Doubling days

Both trend functions append a doubling-days column that estimates how
many days at the current rate it would take for the count to double,
giving an interpretable speed-of-change measure.

## Overview

[csalert](https://niphr.github.io/csalert/) helps create alerts from
public health surveillance data.

# Package index

## All functions

- [`add_holiday_effect()`](https://niphr.github.io/csalert/reference/add_holiday_effect.md)
  : Holiday effect —-
- [`prediction_interval()`](https://niphr.github.io/csalert/reference/prediction_interval.md)
  : Prediction thresholds

  : Prediction thresholds
- [`short_term_trend()`](https://niphr.github.io/csalert/reference/short_term_trend.md)
  : Determine the short term trend of a timeseries
- [`short_term_trend_sts_v1()`](https://niphr.github.io/csalert/reference/short_term_trend_sts_v1.md)
  : Determine the short term trend of a timeseries
- [`signal_detection_hlm()`](https://niphr.github.io/csalert/reference/signal_detection_hlm.md)
  : Determine the short term trend of a timeseries
- [`simulate_baseline_data()`](https://niphr.github.io/csalert/reference/simulate_baseline_data.md)
  : Simulate baseline data —- Simulation of baseline data.
- [`simulate_seasonal_outbreak_data()`](https://niphr.github.io/csalert/reference/simulate_seasonal_outbreak_data.md)
  : Simulate seasonal outbreaks —-
